title: Navigation History, Immutable Snapshots, Back, and Bounded Undo · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for navigation history, immutable snapshots, back, and bounded undo and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded immutable navigation timeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Define back as prior admitted view, not arithmetic or browser history, and expose when no snapshot exists.
Store readonly copied domain snapshots or stable cache identities without shared mutable arrays or pending controllers.

content.text: A quiz TypeScript team handles case 8363 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to verify immutable successful-view history, offline back, stale exclusion, and capacity bounds; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define back as prior admitted view, not arithmetic or browser history, and expose when no snapshot exists.
